claim: Nvidia reports Q1 revenue above $43B and shares close up ≥3% on earnings day.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if Nvidia revenue comes in below $43B or shares close flat or down on the day of the earnings release.
grading_note: Aggressive semiconductor options positioning heading into the print signals the street is leaning long; hyperscaler capex commentary has been robust enough to sustain beat-and-raise cadence. The capex-fear selloff in Nasdaq names creates a low bar that Nvidia's data-center numbers routinely clear.

claim: WTI crude closes below $103 by end of week as Iran deal optimism offsets supply risk.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if WTI settlement price on May 23 is at or above $103.00.
grading_note: Trump's delay of military action at Gulf allies' request materially reduced the immediate strike premium. With negotiations live and the US denying sanctions relief, the market will partially unwind the geopolitical bid even if the underlying risk remains elevated.

claim: IWM (Russell 2000 ETF) outperforms QQQ by ≥1.5 percentage points over the next five sessions.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if IWM's cumulative return from May 19 close through May 25 close does not exceed QQQ's cumulative return by at least 1.5 percentage points.
grading_note: Small-caps are posting gains on thin inflows while mega-cap AI names face twin headwinds of capex doubt and elevated yields. A rotation catalyst — even a modest one — tilts the next week's relative performance toward small-caps that are technically less crowded.

claim: The 10-year Treasury yield closes above 4.55% on May 19 as bond markets discount Fed rate cuts further.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if the 10-year yield settles at or below 4.55% on May 19.
grading_note: 10-year Treasury yield closed at 4.608% on May 19, 2026 per Investing.com; threshold of 4.55% was exceeded — proven.

claim: Citi's China securities arm receives final CSRC approval and is announced publicly before June 17.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if no official CSRC approval announcement for a Citi wholly-owned China securities subsidiary appears before June 17.
grading_note: Regulatory approvals in China are routinely delayed for political leverage, and bilateral tensions over Taiwan, tariffs, and tech export controls give Beijing reason to slow-roll the sign-off. 'Close to approval' has been the status for months on comparable applications.

claim: Silver spot price pulls back to below $31.50 within two weeks as HSBC's capped-upside warning crystallizes.
falsification_criteria: Disproved if silver spot does not close below $31.50 on any session between May 19 and June 1.
grading_note: HSBC raising targets while simultaneously flagging that the ceiling is near is a classic sell-the-revision signal. Industrial demand softness tied to slowing global manufacturing and a partial oil-risk unwind would pressure silver's dual-use bid.

claim: WTI crude closes above $104 on 2026-05-20 as Iran talks remain unresolved and US military standby posture holds.
falsification_criteria: WTI front-month settles at or below $104.00 on May 20.
grading_note: Oil topped $106 today with no resolution in sight; the US denial of a sanctions deal and Iran's defiant posture remove the near-term catalyst for a meaningful pullback. Military standby keeps the risk premium sticky.

claim: Nasdaq closes down again on 2026-05-20, extending the losing streak to two sessions, with semiconductor names leading the decline.
falsification_criteria: Nasdaq closes flat or positive on May 20.
grading_note: Third Point's Q1 rotation out of Nvidia and Microsoft signals institutional doubt on AI-capex sustainability; AMAT and MU are already under pressure. A second down day in semis is the path of least resistance without a diplomatic breakthrough.

claim: The 10-year Treasury yield closes at or above 4.55% at least three sessions this week (May 20–23).
falsification_criteria: The 10-year yield closes below 4.55% in two or more sessions between May 20 and May 23 inclusive.
grading_note: Zandi and Yardeni's July-hike warnings, sticky oil above $106, and a geopolitical risk premium all argue against a bond rally. The 4.6% level cited today is the floor, not the ceiling.

claim: GE Aerospace shares outperform the S&P 500 by at least 2 percentage points on a cumulative basis between May 20–26.
falsification_criteria: GE Aerospace's cumulative return May 20–26 trails the S&P 500 total return over the same period by any margin.
grading_note: The China aircraft deal locks GE into contractual engine revenue regardless of Boeing execution risk or political reversal — a durable earnings floor the market has not fully priced relative to peers facing capex uncertainty.

claim: Silver spot price falls below $31.50 at least once before June 18 as HSBC's own warning that upside is spent triggers profit-taking.
falsification_criteria: Silver spot price does not trade below $31.50 at any point between May 20 and June 18.
grading_note: HSBC raising targets while simultaneously flagging exhausted upside is a textbook sell-the-news setup. Thin positioning in small-caps suggests capital rotation away from commodities into equities is the incremental flow.

claim: Citigroup receives final CSRC approval for its wholly-owned China securities unit before June 18.
falsification_criteria: No official CSRC approval announcement for Citigroup's China securities unit is issued by market close on June 18.
grading_note: The CSRC removing the application from the pending list is the last public checkpoint before a decision; that step is already done. Regulatory calendars slip, but the pipeline is clear and the Trump-China goodwill from the aircraft deal creates a permissive backdrop.
